# Swashbuckler Class Features — Player Core 2 (Remaster)

Source: Player Core 2 pg. 158
Class: Swashbuckler
Key Attribute: Dexterity
Hit Points: 10 plus your Constitution modifier (per level)

---

## Class Progression Table

| Your Level | Class Features |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Ancestry and background, attribute boosts, initial proficiencies, panache, precise strike, stylish combatant, swashbuckler's style, confident finisher, swashbuckler feat |
| 2 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 3 | Fortitude expertise, general feat, opportune riposte, skill increase, stylish tricks, vivacious speed |
| 4 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 5 | Attribute boosts, ancestry feat, precise strike 3 (3d6), skill increase, weapon expertise |
| 6 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 7 | Confident evasion, general feat, skill increase, stylish tricks, vivacious speed, weapon specialization |
| 8 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 9 | Ancestry feat, exemplary finisher, precise strike 4 (4d6), skill increase, swashbuckler expertise |
| 10 | Attribute boosts, skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 11 | Continuous flair, general feat, perception mastery, skill increase, vivacious speed |
| 12 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 13 | Ancestry feat, assured evasion, light armor expertise, precise strike 5 (5d6), skill increase, weapon mastery |
| 14 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 15 | Attribute boosts, general feat, greater weapon specialization, keen flair, skill increase, stylish tricks, vivacious speed |
| 16 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 17 | Ancestry feat, precise strike 6 (6d6), reinforced ego, skill increase |
| 18 | Skill feat, swashbuckler feat |
| 19 | Eternal confidence, general feat, light armor mastery, skill increase, vivacious speed |
| 20 | Attribute boosts, skill feat, swashbuckler feat |

---

## Initial Proficiencies

At 1st level, you gain the listed proficiency ranks in the following statistics. You are untrained in anything not listed unless you gain a better proficiency rank in some other way.

### Perception
- Expert in Perception

### Saving Throws
- Trained in Fortitude
- Expert in Reflex
- Expert in Will

### Skills
- Trained in Acrobatics
- Trained in one skill determined by your swashbuckler's style
- Trained in a number of additional skills equal to 4 plus your Intelligence modifier

### Attacks
- Trained in simple weapons
- Trained in martial weapons
- Trained in unarmed attacks

### Defenses
- Trained in light armor
- Trained in unarmored defense

### Class DC
- Trained in swashbuckler class DC

---

## Key Terms

**Bravado:** Actions with this trait can grant panache, depending on the result of the check involved. If you succeed at the check on a bravado action, you gain panache, and if you fail (but not critically fail) the check, you gain panache but only until the end of your next turn. These effects can be applied even if the action had no other effect due to a failure or a creature's immunity.

**Finisher:** Finishers are spectacular finishing moves that use your panache. Finishers can be used only with weapons that deal additional damage with precise strike (agile or finesse melee weapons or unarmed attacks, for most swashbucklers). You can use a finisher only if you have panache, and you lose your panache immediately after performing a finisher. Once you use a finisher, you can't use actions that have the attack trait for the rest of your turn. Some finisher actions also grant an effect on a failure. Effects added on a failure don't apply on a critical failure. If your finisher action succeeds, you can still choose to apply the failure effect instead. For example, you might do this when an attack deals no damage due to resistance.

**Flourish:** Actions with the flourish trait are special techniques that require too much exertion for you to perform frequently. You can use only one action with the flourish trait per round.

**Stance:** A stance is a general combat strategy that you enter by using an action with the stance trait, and you remain in for some time. A stance lasts until you get knocked out, until its requirements (if any) are violated, until the encounter ends, or until you use a stance action again, whichever comes first. After you take an action with the stance trait, you can't take another one for 1 round. You can enter or be in a stance only in encounter mode. You can Dismiss a stance.

---

## Level 1 Class Features

### Ancestry and Background
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to the abilities provided by your class at 1st level, you have the benefits of your selected ancestry and background.

### Attribute Boosts
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to what you get from your class at 1st level, you have four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. At 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter, you get four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. If an attribute modifier is already +4 or higher, it takes two boosts to increase it; you get a partial boost and must boost that attribute again at a later level to increase it by 1.

### Initial Proficiencies
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 1st level, you gain a number of proficiencies that represent your basic training, noted at the start of this class.

### Panache
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** You care as much about the way you accomplish something as whether you actually accomplish it in the first place. When you perform an action with particular style, you can leverage this moment of verve to perform spectacular, deadly maneuvers. This heightened state of flair is called panache. You gain panache by performing actions that have the bravado trait. Tumble Through and additional actions determined by your swashbuckler's style (see below) gain the bravado trait when you use them. The GM might determine that a check to perform a particularly daring action, such as swinging on a chandelier or sliding down a drapery, can gain the bravado trait. These checks typically involve at least a single action and a non-trivial DC. Normally, you gain and use panache only in combat encounters; when an encounter ends, you lose panache. Powerful finisher actions, including Confident Finisher, can be used only while you have panache and cause you to lose your panache.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Panache is the core loop of the swashbuckler: use bravado actions to gain panache, then spend it on a finisher. The bravado trait (new in the remaster) consolidates how panache is gained — succeed on the check to gain panache, fail (but not critically fail) to gain panache until the end of your next turn. This replaces the pre-remaster system where each style had slightly different gain conditions worded individually. Tumble Through always grants bravado; your style adds more options.

### Precise Strike
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** When you make a Strike with an agile or finesse melee weapon or agile or finesse unarmed attack, you deal 2 additional precision damage. If the Strike is part of a finisher, the additional damage is 2d6 precision damage instead. As your swashbuckler level increases, so does your additional damage for precise strike. At 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level, increase the amount of additional damage on a Strike by 1 and the additional damage on a finisher by 1d6.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Precise strike damage progression: Level 1: +2/2d6 | Level 5: +3/3d6 | Level 9: +4/4d6 | Level 13: +5/5d6 | Level 17: +6/6d6. This is precision damage, so it doesn't apply to creatures immune to precision damage (e.g., swarms, oozes). Only works with agile or finesse melee weapons or unarmed attacks — not ranged weapons unless a feat specifically allows it.

### Stylish Combatant
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks with the bravado trait while in a combat encounter. While you have panache, you gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speeds.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This feature scales via Swashbuckler Expertise (level 9, increases bonus to +2) and Continuous Flair (level 11, extends to exploration mode). The speed bonus from this feature is replaced/upgraded by Vivacious Speed at level 3.

### Swashbuckler's Style
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** Your own distinctive style lets you gracefully handle any situation. Choose a swashbuckler's style. This style determines the additional actions you use to gain panache and makes you trained in the skill related to that action.

#### Battledancer
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Trained Skill:** Performance
- **Bonus Feat:** Fascinating Performance
- **Bravado Actions:** Perform gains the bravado trait.
- **Exemplary Finisher (Level 9):** You can Step as a free action immediately after the finisher.

#### Braggart
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Trained Skill:** Intimidation
- **Bravado Actions:** Demoralize gains the bravado trait.
- **Exemplary Finisher (Level 9):** If the foe was temporarily immune to your Demoralize, their temporary immunity ends.

#### Fencer
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Trained Skill:** Deception
- **Bravado Actions:** Create a Diversion and Feint gain the bravado trait.
- **Exemplary Finisher (Level 9):** The foe is off-guard until your next turn.

#### Gymnast
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Trained Skill:** Athletics
- **Bravado Actions:** Grapple, Reposition, Shove, and Trip gain the bravado trait.
- **Exemplary Finisher (Level 9):** If the foe is grabbed, restrained, or prone, you gain a circumstance bonus to the damage roll equal to double the number of weapon damage dice.

#### Rascal
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Trained Skill:** Thievery
- **Bonus Feat:** Dirty Trick (general feat)
- **Bravado Actions:** Dirty Trick gains the bravado trait.
- **Exemplary Finisher (Level 9):** The foe takes a –10 circumstance penalty to its speed until the start of your next turn.

#### Wit
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Trained Skill:** Diplomacy
- **Bonus Feat:** Bon Mot (skill feat)
- **Bravado Actions:** Bon Mot gains the bravado trait.
- **Exemplary Finisher (Level 9):** The foe takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.

### Confident Finisher
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** 1 action [one-action]
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Description:** You make an incredibly graceful attack, piercing your foe's defenses. Make a Strike with the following failure effect. **Failure:** You deal half your precise strike damage to the target. This damage type is that of the weapon or unarmed attack you used for the Strike.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the swashbuckler's bread-and-butter finisher. Even on a failure, you deal half your precise strike finisher damage (e.g., at level 1, failure deals 1d6 damage; at level 17, failure deals 3d6 damage). This makes it very reliable compared to other martial Strike actions. Requires panache to use; consumes panache on use. Can only be used with weapons that qualify for precise strike (agile or finesse melee weapons/unarmed attacks).

### Swashbuckler Feats
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 1st level and every even-numbered level, you gain a swashbuckler class feat.

---

## Level 2 Class Features

### Skill Feats
- **Level:** 2
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 2nd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill feat. You must be trained or better in the corresponding skill to select a skill feat.

---

## Level 3 Class Features

### Fortitude Expertise
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your physique has grown hardy in your adventures. Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Fortitude is the swashbuckler's weakest save at level 1 (trained), so this shores up a key vulnerability early. It does not increase further through class features — the swashbuckler never gets master or legendary Fortitude from class progression alone.

### General Feats
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level and every 4 levels thereafter, you gain a general feat.

### Opportune Riposte
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Reaction [reaction]
- **Traits:** Bravado, Swashbuckler
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Trigger:** An enemy within your reach critically fails on a Strike against you.
- **Description:** You take advantage of an opening from your enemy's fumbled attack. You either make a melee Strike against the triggering enemy or attempt to Disarm it of the weapon it used for the Strike.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This reaction has the bravado trait, meaning a successful Strike or Disarm check from this reaction can grant you panache. This creates a strong gameplay loop: enemies who critically fail against you give you both a free attack/disarm AND potentially set up your next finisher. The Disarm option uses Athletics vs. Reflex DC.

### Skill Increases
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill increase. You can use this either to increase your proficiency rank to trained in one skill you're untrained in, or to increase your proficiency rank in one skill in which you're already trained to expert. At 7th level, you can use skill increases to increase your proficiency rank to master in a skill in which you're already an expert, and at 15th level, you can use them to increase your proficiency rank to legendary in a skill in which you're already a master.

### Stylish Tricks
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level, 7th level, and 15th level, you gain an additional skill increase you can apply only to Acrobatics or the skill from your swashbuckler's style. You also gain an additional skill feat at these levels. This feat must be for Acrobatics or the trained skill from your swashbuckler's style.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a bonus skill increase AND bonus skill feat at levels 3, 7, and 15 — restricted to Acrobatics or your style's skill. This helps ensure your bravado skill stays competitive as you level. By level 15 with three Stylish Tricks applications plus normal skill increases, you can easily reach legendary in your style skill.

### Vivacious Speed
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** When you've made an impression, you move even faster than normal, darting about the battlefield with incredible speed. Increase the status bonus to your Speeds from stylish combatant to a +10-foot status bonus; this bonus increases by 5 feet at 7th, 11th, 15th, and 19th levels. When you don't have panache, you still get half this status bonus to your Speed, rounded down to the nearest 5-foot increment.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Speed bonus progression (with panache / without panache): Level 3: +10 ft / +5 ft | Level 7: +15 ft / +5 ft | Level 11: +20 ft / +10 ft | Level 15: +25 ft / +10 ft | Level 19: +30 ft / +15 ft. This replaces the +5-foot bonus from Stylish Combatant when you have panache. The "without panache" half bonus is a significant remaster quality-of-life improvement, ensuring mobility even before you've gained panache in a fight.

---

## Level 5 Class Features

### Ancestry Feats
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to the ancestry feat you started with, you gain an ancestry feat at 5th level and every 4 levels thereafter.

### Weapon Expertise
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've dedicated yourself to learning the intricacies of your weapons. Your proficiency ranks for simple weapons, martial weapons, and unarmed attacks increase to expert. You gain access to the critical specialization effects of all weapons for which you have expert proficiency.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Critical specialization effects add extra effects on a critical hit depending on weapon group (e.g., sword group knocks the target off-guard until the start of your next turn, knife group deals persistent bleed damage). This applies to ALL weapons you have expert proficiency in, making the swashbuckler versatile with many weapon types.

---

## Level 7 Class Features

### Confident Evasion
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've learned to move quickly to avoid explosions, a dragon's breath, and worse. Your proficiency rank for Reflex saves increases to master. When you roll a success on a Reflex save, you get a critical success instead.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Master Reflex + success-to-critical-success is Evasion by another name. Combined with the swashbuckler's high Dexterity, this makes them exceptionally hard to catch with area effects. This upgrades from expert (initial proficiency) to master.

### Weapon Specialization
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've learned how to inflict greater injuries with the weapons you know best. You deal 2 additional damage with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you are an expert. This damage increases to 3 if you're a master and 4 if you're legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is flat bonus damage (not precision), so it applies to all hits including against creatures immune to precision damage. Stacks with precise strike damage. At level 7, weapons are expert (+2); at level 13 weapons become master (+3).

---

## Level 9 Class Features

### Exemplary Finisher
- **Level:** 9
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You execute your finishing moves with spectacular flair, adding special effects to your finishers. If a Strike you make as part of a finisher hits a foe, you add one of the following effects to the Strike, depending on your swashbuckler's style:
  - **Battledancer:** You can Step as a free action immediately after the finisher.
  - **Braggart:** If the foe was temporarily immune to your Demoralize, their temporary immunity ends.
  - **Fencer:** The foe is off-guard until your next turn.
  - **Gymnast:** If the foe is grabbed, restrained, or prone, you gain a circumstance bonus to the damage roll equal to double the number of weapon damage dice.
  - **Rascal:** The foe takes a –10 circumstance penalty to its speed until the start of your next turn.
  - **Wit:** The foe takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against you until the start of your next turn.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This adds a free rider effect whenever your finisher Strike hits. These effects are powerful and style-defining: Fencer gets free off-guard (great for party synergy), Braggart can re-Demoralize the same target immediately (bypassing the normal 10-minute immunity), Gymnast gets massive bonus damage on prone/grabbed foes, etc. The effect triggers on a hit, and since Confident Finisher's failure effect counts as dealing damage (not a hit), the exemplary finisher effect only triggers on an actual success or critical success.

### Swashbuckler Expertise
- **Level:** 9
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You perform swashbuckling techniques with exceptional flair, making them harder to resist. Your circumstance bonus from Stylish Combatant increases to +2. Your proficiency rank for your swashbuckler class DC increases to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** The circumstance bonus to bravado skill checks goes from +1 to +2, making it significantly easier to succeed on bravado actions and thus gain panache. Expert class DC improves the DCs of any abilities that use your swashbuckler class DC.

---

## Level 11 Class Features

### Continuous Flair
- **Level:** 11
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** While not equal to your panache in combat, you have a dramatic flair about you in any situation. The circumstance bonus from Stylish Combatant applies in exploration mode.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Before this feature, the circumstance bonus to bravado skill checks only applied in combat encounters. Now the +2 bonus (from Swashbuckler Expertise) applies during exploration mode as well, benefiting Acrobatics, Intimidation, Performance, and other style skills used outside of combat.

### Perception Mastery
- **Level:** 11
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Through your adventures, you've developed keen awareness and attention to detail. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Upgrades from expert (initial) to master. Swashbucklers are among the better Perception classes, starting at expert and reaching master by 11. This helps with initiative (which typically uses Perception) and detecting hidden or invisible creatures.

---

## Level 13 Class Features

### Assured Evasion
- **Level:** 13
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your ability to elude danger is matchless. Your proficiency rank for Reflex saves increases to legendary. When you roll a critical failure on a Reflex save, you get a failure instead. When you roll a failure on a Reflex save against a damaging effect, you take half damage.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Legendary Reflex + critical-failure-to-failure + half-damage-on-failure makes the swashbuckler virtually immune to Reflex-based damage in most situations. Combined with Confident Evasion (success → critical success), the progression is: critical failure → failure, failure → half damage, success → critical success. This is one of the strongest Reflex save progressions in the game.

### Light Armor Expertise
- **Level:** 13
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've learned to dodge while wearing light or no armor. Your proficiency ranks for light armor and unarmored defense increase to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Upgrades from trained to expert. This comes relatively late compared to some other martial classes. The swashbuckler relies more on mobility and Reflex saves than raw AC.

### Weapon Mastery
- **Level:** 13
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You fully understand your weapons. Your proficiency ranks for simple and martial weapons and unarmed attacks increase to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Upgrades from expert (level 5) to master. This also increases weapon specialization damage from +2 (expert) to +3 (master). Master proficiency weapons also qualify for Keen Flair at level 15.

---

## Level 15 Class Features

### Greater Weapon Specialization
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your damage from weapon specialization increases to 4 with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you're an expert, 6 if you're a master, and 8 if you're legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** At level 15, weapons are master proficiency, so this grants +6 flat damage per hit. This is a significant damage boost that stacks with precise strike.

### Keen Flair
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You inflict devastating attacks on even well-defended foes. When you Strike with a weapon or unarmed attack with which you have master proficiency, if you roll a 19 on the die and the roll is a success, you critically succeed instead.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the "keen" effect — natural 19 counts as a critical hit if the result would be a success. Since weapons reach master at level 13, this applies to all simple/martial weapons and unarmed attacks. Doubles your critical hit chance on attacks that would normally succeed.

---

## Level 17 Class Features

### Reinforced Ego
- **Level:** 17
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** You've steeled your mind with resolve. Your proficiency rank for Will saves increases to master. When you roll a success on a Will save, you get a critical success instead.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Will saves upgrade from expert (initial) to master, plus success-to-critical-success. This gives the swashbuckler strong mental defenses at high levels. The success-to-critical-success effect is particularly powerful against incapacitation effects and conditions like frightened or confused.

---

## Level 19 Class Features

### Eternal Confidence
- **Level:** 19
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 163
- **Description:** As a swashbuckler at the peak of your skill, you swell with confidence in every attack. Your proficiency rank for your swashbuckler class DC increases to master. When you Strike as part of a finisher or Opportune Riposte, you can give the Strike the failure effect from the Confident Finisher action, including the increase from Precise Finisher if you have that feat. You can do so only if the Strike uses a weapon or unarmed attack that you could use for Confident Finisher.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a capstone-tier feature that adds Confident Finisher's failure effect to ALL finisher Strikes and Opportune Riposte Strikes. This means every finisher and riposte you make deals at minimum half your precise strike finisher damage (3d6 at this level) even on a miss. Master class DC also improves any class DC-based abilities. If you have Precise Finisher feat, the failure effect improves to full precise strike damage instead of half.

### Light Armor Mastery
- **Level:** 19
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** Your skill with light armor improves, increasing your ability to dodge blows. Your proficiency ranks for light armor and unarmored defense increase to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Upgrades from expert (level 13) to master. This provides a final AC boost at the very end of the class progression. Master armor proficiency is the highest the swashbuckler achieves — they never reach legendary armor.

---

## Proficiency Progression Summary

| Statistic | Level 1 | Level 3 | Level 5 | Level 7 | Level 9 | Level 11 | Level 13 | Level 15 | Level 17 | Level 19 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Perception | Expert | — | — | — | — | Master | — | — | — | — |
| Fortitude | Trained | Expert | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| Reflex | Expert | — | — | Master | — | — | Legendary | — | — | — |
| Will | Expert | — | — | — | — | — | — | — | Master | — |
| Simple/Martial/Unarmed | Trained | — | Expert | — | — | — | Master | — | — | — |
| Light Armor / Unarmored | Trained | — | — | — | — | — | Expert | — | — | Master |
| Class DC | Trained | — | — | — | Expert | — | — | — | — | Master* |

*Class DC reaches master via Eternal Confidence at level 19.

## Precise Strike Damage Progression

| Level | Flat Bonus (per Strike) | Finisher Bonus |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | +2 precision | 2d6 precision |
| 5 | +3 precision | 3d6 precision |
| 9 | +4 precision | 4d6 precision |
| 13 | +5 precision | 5d6 precision |
| 17 | +6 precision | 6d6 precision |

---

## Vivacious Speed Progression

| Level | With Panache | Without Panache |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 (Stylish Combatant) | +5 ft | +0 ft |
| 3 | +10 ft | +5 ft |
| 7 | +15 ft | +5 ft |
| 11 | +20 ft | +10 ft |
| 15 | +25 ft | +10 ft |
| 19 | +30 ft | +15 ft |

---

## Notable Remaster Changes (Player Core 2 vs. Advanced Player's Guide)

1. **Bravado Trait (NEW):** The remaster introduces the bravado trait as a unified mechanic for gaining panache. Previously, each style described its own panache-gaining conditions inline. Now, actions that can grant panache simply have the bravado trait, and the trait itself defines the success/failure outcomes (succeed = panache; fail but not critically fail = panache until end of next turn).

2. **Stylish Combatant (NEW name):** This feature consolidates the circumstance bonus to bravado checks and the panache speed bonus into a single named feature. Previously these were part of "Panache" description.

3. **Confident Evasion (renamed):** Was previously "Evasion" — renamed to Confident Evasion for thematic flavor but functions identically (master Reflex, success → critical success).

4. **Assured Evasion (renamed):** Was previously "Improved Evasion" — renamed. Functionally similar but now also explicitly grants half damage on failure against damaging Reflex effects.

5. **Reinforced Ego (renamed):** Was previously "Resolve" — renamed to a swashbuckler-themed name. Functions identically (master Will, success → critical success).

6. **Rascal style:** Uses the new Dirty Trick general feat as its bravado action (replaces the pre-remaster version that used Feint from Thievery). Grants the Dirty Trick feat for free.

7. **Gymnast style expanded:** Reposition is now included alongside Grapple, Shove, and Trip as bravado actions (Reposition is a new remastered Athletics action).

8. **Attribute terminology:** "Ability scores" are now "attribute modifiers" throughout — this is a system-wide remaster change.

9. **Off-guard replaces flat-footed:** The condition previously called "flat-footed" is now "off-guard" — relevant to Fencer style's Exemplary Finisher.

10. **Eternal Confidence refined:** Now explicitly mentions compatibility with Precise Finisher feat and clarifies it works with Opportune Riposte Strikes as well.

---
